macro_context.headline: Quiet Monday session with US markets closed; gold holds above $4,500 as oil pulls back on Hormuz de-escalation signals.

EURUSD.name: Euro / US Dollar
EURUSD.past: EUR/USD held 1.1737 as the DXY slipped to 98.10 — dollar broadly soft ahead of Friday's US payroll print expected at just 50K vs April's 178K.
EURUSD.why: The weak-dollar thesis is the week's dominant trade; if payrolls confirm the slowdown, Fed rate-cut bets accelerate and EUR/USD extends toward 1.19.
EURUSD.watch: Tuesday US ISM Services and Wednesday ADP are the first checkpoints — a sub-50K payrolls print Friday is the binary catalyst for a EUR/USD breakout above 1.18.

headlines.title: ECB, Bank of England Set to Hold Rates Unchanged Today
Eni Raises Share Buyback Plan to $3.3B
Dollar softens but heads for weekly gain amid Middle East uncertainty

GBPUSD.name: British Pound / US Dollar
GBPUSD.past: GBP/USD dipped to 1.3592, underperforming EUR slightly as sterling-specific flows dominated — UK construction PMI data came in mixed.
GBPUSD.why: The pound lags the broader dollar-weakness move because sticky UK services inflation keeps the BoE cautious; the rate differential to EUR narrows slowly.
GBPUSD.watch: Thursday BoE rate decision — any signal of a June cut sends GBP below 1.35; a hold or hawkish tone targets a run back to 1.37.

headlines.title: Hedge Funds Are Shorting the U.S. Dollar So Make This 1 Trade Now
FTSE 100 today: UK stocks gain, pound above $1.35 amid U.S.-Iran talks hopes
How to avoid being caught out by changes to the state pension

USDJPY.name: US Dollar / Japanese Yen
USDJPY.past: USD/JPY edged down to 156.84 as US 10Y yields fell 2.7 bps to 4.38% and the DXY softened — yen gained marginal ground but no breakout.
USDJPY.why: The BOJ rate-hike path is slow but real; the narrowing US-Japan rate differential is a structural yen tailwind, currently masking any break below 156.
USDJPY.watch: Friday US payrolls — a sub-50K print breaks 156 support and targets 154.80; a beat re-loads the carry trade and pushes USD/JPY back toward 158.

headlines.title: Why a Plunging Dollar Is Missing Out on the Relief Rally
How Markets Reacted to Trump's Iran Post, in Charts
The Dollar Isn’t Done. Why It’s Outshining Rivals Amid Iran Crisis.

AUDUSD.name: Australian Dollar / US Dollar
AUDUSD.past: AUD/USD barely moved at 0.7207, caught between a soft DXY tailwind and pre-RBA positioning — traders reluctant to commit ahead of Tuesday's decision.
AUDUSD.why: Markets price roughly 50% probability of an RBA 25bp cut; a hold would be a sharp AUD positive, a cut would validate the bears who see 0.70 ahead.
AUDUSD.watch: RBA decision Tuesday approximately 04:30 TRT — cut targets 0.7120, hold squeezes shorts toward 0.7280. Binary event; sizing matters.

headlines.title: Why is the Australian Dollar Rallying?
The Next Trade You Should Make Comes from the Land Down Under
Dollar subdued as markets eye ceasefire talks; yen pressured by BOJ delay

XAUUSD.name: Gold
XAUUSD.past: Gold pulled back to $4,601, shedding $29 as the acute Iran risk premium faded and yields found a floor — consolidation after an extraordinary run above $4,600.
XAUUSD.why: Gold needs either a payrolls miss (accelerating Fed cut bets) or a fresh geopolitical escalation to make the next leg higher; pure haven demand is cooling for now.
XAUUSD.watch: Friday US payrolls — a sub-50K miss revives $4,700 as the next target; a hawkish surprise (yields up, dollar up) opens the $4,450-$4,500 support band.

headlines.title: Markets on alert as Trump vows ‘Project Freedom’ for Hormuz, setting up potential showdown after renewed attacks on ships
Gold dips as inflation concerns linger; markets watch US-Iran talks
This Natural Resources ETF Has Silently Outperformed The S&P 500 Year-To-Date While Yielding 5%

XAGUSD.name: Silver
XAGUSD.past: Silver dropped to $75.37, underperforming gold as the gold/silver ratio widens — a signal that industrial demand optimism is softening.
XAGUSD.why: China's mainland markets close May 1-5 for Labor Day, removing a key physical buyer from the market; silver's industrial demand proxy suffers most.
XAGUSD.watch: Wednesday China services PMI (markets reopen May 6) — a strong print above 52 tightens the gold/silver ratio and revives $77-$78 targets.

headlines.title: American West secures A$10m placement to accelerate West Desert drilling and fund growth projects
Citi Bank has a message for investors on gold
Check Your Wallet—Here's What's Actually Worth Money in Your Spare Change

USOIL.name: US Oil (WTI)
USOIL.past: WTI slid to $101.62 as the immediate Iran escalation risk fades — still comfortably above the $100 psychological mark, its first sustained hold there since 2022.
USOIL.why: OPEC+ shows no appetite to accelerate output hikes; the $100+ floor is structural until either Iran tensions fully defuse or global demand data weakens sharply.
USOIL.watch: Wednesday EIA crude inventory report — a draw above 2M barrels re-tests $105; a build above 3M barrels would put $98 support in play.

headlines.title: Higher gas prices are eating into Americans' tax refunds
European Value Stocks Priced Below Estimated Worth In May 2026
European Dividend Stocks To Consider For Your Portfolio

US500.name: S&P 500
US500.past: S&P 500 edged to 7,230 — Apple's record quarter (+3.2%) and Big Tech earnings beats sustained the index near all-time highs; Goldman Sachs lifted its S&P year-end target.
US500.why: Mega-cap earnings delivered, compressing the equity risk premium; the AI infrastructure cycle is self-reinforcing and the breadth of beats reduces single-name risk.
US500.watch: Friday April payrolls (consensus 50K vs 178K prior) — a weak print accelerates Fed cut bets and is bullish; a beat above 100K risks a 2-3% pullback on hawkish re-pricing.

headlines.title: Jobs day, semiconductor earnings, and stock market momentum: What to watch this week
'It's a boom': Wall Street sees more market gains as strong earnings fuel the AI trade
Veeva Systems (VEEV) Is Up 6.9% After S&P 500 Addition and GC Transition News

NAS100.name: Nasdaq 100
NAS100.past: Nasdaq 100 gained 0.94% to 27,710 — best session of the week — as Apple, Microsoft, and Amazon posted earnings-driven gains and hyperscaler capex plans impressed.
NAS100.why: MSFT, GOOGL, and AMZN all showed accelerating cloud growth driven by AI workloads, validating the infrastructure investment thesis that has taken the Nasdaq to records.
NAS100.watch: NVDA's late-May earnings is the next major Nasdaq catalyst; between now and then, Friday payrolls is the key macro risk — a strong print risks sector rotation out of tech.

headlines.title: Stock Futures Are Rising, Oil Falls Ahead of Jobs Week
2 Nasdaq 100 Stocks with Solid Fundamentals and 1 We Find Risky
S&P 500 and Nasdaq 100 Post Record Highs on Solid Earnings

US30.name: Dow Jones Industrial Average
US30.past: Dow Jones slipped to 49,499 as its value-heavy composition lagged the tech-led rally — energy and financials weighed while Nasdaq surged.
US30.why: Value-to-growth rotation continues when hyperscaler earnings dominate — the Dow underperforms Nasdaq in AI-driven sessions, a pattern persisting for several quarters.
US30.watch: 50,000 is the psychological resistance; a payrolls miss on Friday boosts rate-cut bets, potentially rotating money back into financials and closing the Nasdaq/Dow gap.

headlines.title: Jobs day, semiconductor earnings, and stock market momentum: What to watch this week
Dow Jones Futures: Trump Says U.S. To 'Guide' Ships Through Hormuz
Markets on alert as Trump vows ‘Project Freedom’ for Hormuz, setting up potential showdown after renewed attacks on ships

BTC.name: Bitcoin
BTC.past: BTC bounced to $79,703 (+1.5%) with BTC dominance at 58.5% — the 'great rotation back to crypto' narrative gaining traction as equity highs lift risk appetite broadly.
BTC.why: BTC still trails its $97K January peak by ~18%; the rotation narrative requires sustained equity stability and a risk-on macro backdrop — both currently present but fragile.
BTC.watch: $80,000 is key resistance; a daily close above it targets $85K. Friday's payrolls miss (risk-on) accelerates the move; a beat (yields up) fades BTC toward $77K.

headlines.title: Bitcoin Tops $80,000, Reaching Three-Month High as Stocks Rise

Don't Buy Hyperliquid If This Happens

ETH.name: Ethereum
ETH.past: ETH gained 1.73% to $2,362, outpacing BTC — BitMine announced a $5M ETH treasury purchase, adding institutional demand signal; a BTC/ETH dominance death cross flagged by analysts.
ETH.why: The ETH dominance death cross suggests potential rotation from BTC into ETH; Ethereum's growing L2 ecosystem and staking yield are improving the relative fundamental case.
ETH.watch: $2,400 is the first resistance level; a breakout targets $2,600 and would signal a genuine altcoin rotation cycle beginning. Watch BTC dominance — if it drops below 57%, ETH accelerates.

headlines.title: Tired of Being Burned by Crypto? Cardano Targets Long‑Term Stability Over Meme Cycles.
Is Ethereum the Generational Wealth Pick for Real‑World Crypto Use?
Ethereum Exit Queue Explodes 72,000% After DeFi Hack Wave

NVDA.name: NVIDIA
NVDA.past: NVDA slipped 0.56% to $198.45 — the lone notable decliner in Big Tech — consolidating as hyperscalers reaffirmed strong AI infrastructure spending but no NVDA-specific catalyst fired.
NVDA.why: Hyperscaler capex beats are bullish for NVDA's forward pipeline but the market already expects it; the stock needs its own earnings to re-rate above $200 sustainably.
NVDA.watch: $200 is the resistance that matters; clearing it on volume targets $215. Late May earnings are the binary catalyst — any pre-announcement or export restriction news is the short-term risk.

headlines.title: Interactive Brokers Is Winning From Market Volatility. Can It Keep Going?

Don't Buy Hyperliquid If This Happens

TSLA.name: Tesla
TSLA.past: TSLA surged 2.41% to $390.82 as Musk testified before Congress and Amazon + Tesla were cited as driving one-third of FDI inflows into the US — brand and macro tailwind combined.
TSLA.why: TSLA trades more like an AI/autonomy platform than a car company now; Musk's FDI narrative and institutional attention keep the stock elevated well above traditional auto valuations.
TSLA.watch: $400 is the next round-number resistance — a break above marks the highest level since late 2024 and invites momentum buying. Musk headlines remain the dominant near-term risk factor.

headlines.title: 5 things we learned during S&P 500 earnings madness
SpaceX will pay Elon Musk a bonus if he gets at least 1 million people to live on Mars — here's the plan
Is Tesla, Inc. (TSLA) A Good Stock To Buy Now?

AAPL.name: Apple
AAPL.past: Apple surged 3.24% to $280.14 after reporting a record quarter — services revenue hit new highs and iPhone demand beat analyst estimates despite supply chain concerns.
AAPL.why: Apple's services business (App Store, payments, streaming) is now the real growth engine: high-margin, recurring, and globally scaling — it's becoming a quality compounder, not just a hardware company.
AAPL.watch: $285 is the next resistance and gap-fill target; tariff or India supply chain headline is the key risk to fade this move. Short-term support sits at $273.

Seoul, Taipei hit records as Asian stocks track Wall St tech rally

MSFT.name: Microsoft
MSFT.past: Microsoft gained 1.63% to $414.44 as Azure cloud growth and AI Copilot adoption impressed — the hyperscaler capex cycle reaffirmed MSFT as the institutional AI proxy.
MSFT.why: Azure beat estimates again; the feedback loop is self-reinforcing: more AI demand drives more Azure revenue, which funds more R&D, which attracts more enterprise customers.
MSFT.watch: $420 is the next price target cluster; key risk is a macro shock (payrolls beat, geopolitics) triggering broad tech rotation. Support at $408 on any pullback.

headlines.title: 'It's a boom': Wall Street sees more market gains as strong earnings fuel the AI trade
This Major Tech Stock Could Be the Biggest Loser From AI
Musk vs OpenAI trial enters second week

META.name: Meta Platforms
META.past: Meta slipped 0.52% to $608.75, digesting last week's earnings — Reality Labs losses offsetting strong AI-driven ad growth was flagged as the key concern in the quarter.
META.why: The advertising flywheel is intact with AI-targeted ad precision improving, but concentration risk is elevated — META and one peer drive the entire Communication Services ETF.
META.watch: $600 is key support; a hold confirms the post-earnings dip as a buy opportunity. A close below $600 targets $575 and would signal institutional de-risking of the position.

headlines.title: This Major Tech Stock Could Be the Biggest Loser From AI
AI Capex Boom Drives Hottest ETF Trade Into Semiconductors, Not Crypto
Jim Cramer on Meta: “It’s Being Clubbed Like a Baby Seal”

GOOGL.name: Alphabet (Google)
GOOGL.past: Alphabet gained 0.23% to $385.69, buoyed by hyperscaler earnings season — Google Cloud beat targets and YouTube ad revenue growth confirmed AI-driven ad monetization is accelerating.
GOOGL.why: Near-term fundamentals are strong with cloud and ads accelerating, but the structural risk — AI chatbots eroding Google Search share — is the valuation ceiling that keeps the stock range-bound.
GOOGL.watch: $390 is the next resistance; AI search disruption headlines (ChatGPT search share data, regulatory) are the primary risk. Support at $376.

headlines.title: This Major Tech Stock Could Be the Biggest Loser From AI
Dow Jones Futures: Trump Says U.S. To 'Guide' Ships Through Hormuz
Seoul, Taipei hit records as Asian stocks track Wall St tech rally

AMZN.name: Amazon
AMZN.past: Amazon gained 1.21% to $268.26; BofA revised its price target upward after AWS growth and retail margins both impressed — Amazon and Tesla cited as driving one-third of US FDI.
AMZN.why: AWS is re-accelerating on AI workload demand; combined with expanding retail margins, this is arguably the strongest near-term fundamental story in mega-cap tech.
AMZN.watch: $275 is the next price target cluster from street analysts; FTC or EU regulatory news is the key downside risk. AWS growth rate deceleration (reported in next quarter) would be the structural warning.
